title: celtis caucasica subsp. caudata
intro: Celtis caucasica subsp. caudata, also known as Caucasian hackberry or Caucasian winged elm, is a deciduous tree species within the Celtis genus. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, primarily due to its high concentration of flavonoids, alkaloids, and other bioactive compounds, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Celtis caucasica subsp. caudata include its potential to reduce blood pressure, alleviate respiratory issues, and exhibit hepatoprotective properties, among others. In herbal preparations, the bark, leaves, and roots of this plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are traditionally used to treat various health conditions and promote overall well-being.
